content_preview.title: EU Faces Defining DMA Review as Brussels Weighs AI and Cloud Expansion Amid Escalating US Tariff Threats
content_preview.summary: The European Commission must deliver its first mandatory review of the Digital Markets Act by May 3, weighing calls to expand the law into AI and cloud services while fending off Trump administration tariff threats over $7 billion in cumulative tech fines.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The European Commission is approaching what may be the most consequential moment in the short history of the Digital Markets Act. By May 3, 2026, the Commission is legally required to deliver its first triennial review of the DMA to the European Parliament, the Council of the European Union, and the European Economic and Social Committee, according to the [Commission's own review timeline](https://digital-markets-act.ec.europa.eu/commission-publishes-summary-and-responses-consultati...

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong policy Analysis on EU DMA triennial review.
editor_notes.source_verification: EU Commission DMA portal VERIFIED via WebFetch: May 3 2026 review deadline, July 2025 public consultation, 450+ submissions, AI/cloud scope expansion requests, gatekeeper objections, three-year mandatory review all confirmed.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: DMA review timeline, consultation process, scope expansion debate verified.
